In a large rondeau or saute pan, toast the pine nuts in the butter until golden brown.
Remove from heat, strain and reserve the butter and pine nuts seperately.  Cool pine nuts on sheet tray.
In a robot coup, process half of the pine nuts just before it begins to cream (should be crumbles still)
Combine the whole and processed pine nuts in a mixing bowl.
In a robot coup, process the raisins, then add to the mixing bowl with the pine nuts
Add all other ingredients, including reserved butter.
Mix with gloved hands, ensure raisins are broken up and mixture is homogenous.
Reserve in quart containers and store in the cooler.
